subject: Anthropology
title: Phonology
description: A short lesson to help you apply Phonology.

hook.text: In English, R and L are different sounds. In Japanese, they're the same. Your language trains your ears.

buildup.text: Phonology is the study of sound systems — the meaningful sound units (phonemes) that a language uses.

discovery.text: Every language selects from a limited set of possible human sounds. No language uses them all.

twist.text: Babies hear all sounds at birth. By twelve months, they've tuned in to their native language and tuned out the rest.

climax.text: Phonology shows that even hearing is cultural. Your language literally shapes what sounds you can perceive.

punchline.text: You hear what your language taught you to hear.

quiz.question: What is a phoneme?
options: A meaningful sound unit in a language
